title: Leave-one-out peer pressure
summary: Estimate how much pressure other cars on the same race lap are under, excluding the current car.
rationale: Several peer-pressure variants were attempted; the mechanism is relevant because pit windows often occur in waves. Leave-one-out aggregation avoids letting the row’s own pressure dominate its peer signal.
implementation_hint: For each race-lap group, compute group sums/counts for key binary and continuous pressure features, subtract the current row’s value, and divide by the number of peers. Add peer mean pressure, peer high-pressure rate, and own-minus-peer pressure deltas.
expected_effect: Small ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Groups with one car need safe defaults; past attempts failed when grouped Series/DataFrame indexes were misaligned.
